title: Plan Captions, Transcripts, and Audio Description
summary: Plan Captions, Transcripts, and Audio Description retrieves prior skills in an original heat-advisory media equivalence editorial desk scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply accessible media equivalents in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real heat-advisory media equivalence editorial desk artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

steps.title: 04 · Read the heat-advisory media equivalence editorial desk contract · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which practice survives changed data and devices? Scenario: heat-advisory media equivalence editorial desk.
steps.why: Plan Captions, Transcripts, and Audio Description makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in heat-advisory media equivalence editorial desk.

content.text: Captions communicate dialogue and meaningful sound; transcripts provide an equivalent text path; audio description conveys important visual information; and text or image alternatives must preserve the task-relevant meaning. Automatic output requires human review for timing, speakers, terminology, and critical sounds. Controls must remain operable and media must not autoplay unexpectedly. Apply it to heat-advisory media equivalence editorial desk.
